# Syllabus Eindtermen — Machine-Extracted Register

**This file is machine-generated.** Source: `references/external/syllabus-economie-vwo-2026-versie-2.pdf` (CvTE official syllabus). Regenerated by `build-scripts/references/extract-eindtermen.js`. Do not hand-edit.

A companion `syllabus-eindtermen.json` is produced alongside this file and is what code consumes (e.g., the unit-registry validator checks that every `exam_codes` entry resolves to a real eindterm here).

---

## Format specification

Each eindterm entry follows this exact structure:

```markdown
### <code>
- Domein: <letter> (<domein title>)
- Subdomein: <code> (<subdomein title>)
- Tekst: "<verbatim eindterm text from the CvTE PDF>"
- Verb: <primary verb, e.g. herkennen / uitleggen / berekenen / analyseren>
- Implied bloom: <remember | understand | apply | analyze | evaluate>
- Examen: <centraal | schoolexamen>
- Year: <syllabus year, e.g. 2026>
```

### Code format

`<DomainLetter><SubdomainDigit>.<EindtermDigit>[<suffix>]`, where the suffix is a lowercase letter used for sub-items within a single eindterm. Examples from the 2026 syllabus:

- `D1.1` — one-part eindterm
- `D1.4a`, `D1.4b` — two-part eindterm
- `H1.1` — macro domain
- `A4.2` — vaardigheden domain, rekenen subdomein

### Verb → Bloom mapping (used to populate `Implied bloom`)

| CvTE verb | Implied bloom |
|---|---|
| herkennen, benoemen | remember |
| uitleggen, beschrijven, verklaren | understand |
| berekenen, toepassen, afleiden | apply |
| analyseren, onderscheiden, classificeren | analyze |
| beoordelen, evalueren | evaluate |

Multi-verb eindtermen use the highest-Bloom verb. E.g. "De kandidaat kan X berekenen en beoordelen" → `evaluate`.

### Centraal examen vs. schoolexamen

| Domein | Level |
|---|---|
| A (Vaardigheden) | both (cross-cutting) |
| B (Schaarste), C (Ruil) | schoolexamen only |
| D (Markt), E (Ruilen over tijd), F (Samenwerken), G (Risico), H (Welvaart), I (Goede/slechte tijden) | centraal examen |
| J (Onderzoek), K (Keuzeonderwerpen) | schoolexamen only |

The extraction script populates the `Examen:` field automatically from the domain letter.

---

## Example entries (illustrative, not authoritative)

```markdown
### D1.1
- Domein: D (Concept markt)
- Subdomein: D1 (Vraag)
- Tekst: "Betalingsbereidheid als de maximale prijs die een vrager bereid is te betalen voor één eenheid van een goed."
- Verb: herkennen
- Implied bloom: remember
- Examen: centraal
- Year: 2026

### D1.4a
- Domein: D (Concept markt)
- Subdomein: D1 (Vraag)
- Tekst: "Het verband tussen de betalingsbereidheid van alle vragers en het verloop van de collectieve vraaglijn."
- Verb: herkennen
- Implied bloom: understand
- Examen: centraal
- Year: 2026

### A4.1
- Domein: A (Vaardigheden)
- Subdomein: A4 (Rekenen)
- Tekst: "De kandidaat kan rekenkundig onderbouwd economische situaties analyseren."
- Verb: analyseren
- Implied bloom: analyze
- Examen: centraal (cross-cutting)
- Year: 2026
```

The authoritative entries will be produced by running `extract-eindtermen.js`. The above are illustrative only.

---

## How this register is consumed

1. **Unit-registry validator** (`build-scripts/references/build-unit-index.js`) — every entry in a unit's `exam_codes:` list must resolve to a real eindterm code here, otherwise the write fails.
2. **Coverage report** (`reports/exam-coverage.md`) — joins eindtermen with units to show coverage.
3. **Gap reports** (`reports/exam-vs-program-gaps.md`) — eindtermen with no exam-question audit evidence surface as "program-only ghosts"; skills observed in exam questions without a clean eindterm link surface as "exam-only signal".
4. **Yearly delta** (`reports/exam-program-delta.md`) — diffs the new extraction against a committed prior-year snapshot to make curriculum changes visible in review.

---
